result:
Q4 integrated (FR-022 expanded).

---

**Q5: Warm-up ramp scheme (needs a defined rule to be unit-testable)**

The warm-up ramp is auto-generated to the working weight and must be unit-tested, so it needs a deterministic rule. The question is what the ramp computes from and how many steps.

**Recommended:** Option A — a fixed percentage ladder off the working weight, plate-rounded, with the empty bar first. It's deterministic, testable, scales to any lift, and respects the same inventory-loadable rounding as prescriptions (Q1).

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| A | Percentage ladder of the working weight — e.g., empty bar → ~40% → 60% → 80%, each plate-rounded to loadable weight, descending reps; skipped for very light working weights below a threshold |
| B | Fixed absolute jumps from the bar (e.g., +45 each set) until near the working weight |
| C | Program-defined warm-up where the program specifies one, else the percentage-ladder default |
| Short | Different scheme (≤5 words) |

You can reply with the option letter, accept the recommendation ("yes"/"recommended"), or provide your own short answer. (This is the last planned question.)
